16.reason: [This loop overwrites 'xs' each iteration; should count total fractional digits, not just last index difference]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop overwrites 'xs' in every iteration, so after the loop, 'xs' will be equal to (last_index - zs), not the total number of fractional digits. It should be 'xs = strlen(num) - zs - 1;' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop overwrites 'xs' in every iteration, so after the loop, 'xs' will be equal to (last_index - zs), not the total number of fractional digits. It should count the number of digits after the decimal point instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop overwrites 'xs' in every iteration, so after the loop, 'xs' will be equal to (last_index - zs), not the total number of fractional digits. It should count the number of digits after the decimal point, but instead it ends up as (strlen(num)-1 - zs). This leads to incorrect exponent calculation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop overwrites 'xs' each iteration; should count total fractional digits, not just last index difference]

28.reason: [This condition assumes 'xs' correctly represents the number of digits after the decimal point, but due to the earlier error in computing 'xs', this logic is flawed. Also, 'xs-1' may be negative or incorrect.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition assumes 'xs' correctly represents the number of digits after the decimal point, but due to the earlier error in computing 'xs', this logic is flawed. Also, 'xs-1' may be negative or incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Because 'xs' is incorrectly computed above, this condition may evaluate incorrectly. For example, if input is "0.001", 'xs' becomes 3 (since last i=4, zs=1, xs=4-1=3), but actual fractional digits = 3, trailing non-zero at position 4. However, 'zero' counts leading zeros after '.', which is 2. Then 'zero == xs-1' 鈫 2 == 2 鈫 true, which is correct in this case, but logic is fragile due to wrong 'xs'.]
